source_constraint: All facts must be verifiable against the MystenLabs/walrus-sites repo and docs.wal.app/docs/sites.

evals.prompt: I built a React app with Vite. How do I deploy it to Walrus Sites on testnet?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step: npm run build, then site-builder publish --epochs 30 dist/. Mentions prerequisites and that testnet requires a local portal.
expectations: Instructs to build the frontend first (npm run build or equivalent)
Uses site-builder publish with --epochs flag and a value of 30 or higher
Points site-builder at the dist/ directory, not the source directory
Mentions suiup install site-builder for installation
Mentions that wal.app only serves mainnet, testnet needs a self-hosted portal

evals.prompt: I used --epochs 5 when publishing my site. Is that enough?
evals.expected_output: No, that's far too low. Blobs expire within days. Use --epochs 30+ for testnet, 200+ or --permanent for mainnet.
expectations: Clearly states --epochs 5 is too low and will expire quickly
Recommends --epochs 30 or higher for testnet
Recommends higher values (200+) or --permanent for mainnet
Mentions site-builder sitemap to check expiration dates
Explains that expired blobs cause the site to 404 silently

evals.prompt: I have a React SPA deployed on Walrus Sites. When I navigate directly to /dashboard it shows a 404. The homepage works fine.
evals.expected_output: SPA routing issue. Add a routes section to ws-resources.json mapping /* to /index.html.
expectations: Identifies this as an SPA routing issue, not a deployment bug
Explains that the portal looks up the exact path as a resource
Provides the ws-resources.json routes configuration with /* mapping to /index.html
Instructs to re-publish or update after modifying ws-resources.json

evals.prompt: What's the difference between site-builder publish and site-builder update?
evals.expected_output: publish creates a new site object; update modifies an existing one using ws-resources.json. Only changed resources are re-uploaded during update.
expectations: Explains publish creates a new site object on Sui
Explains update modifies an existing site (requires ws-resources.json with object ID)
Mentions that update only re-uploads changed resources
Notes that ws-resources.json is auto-generated on first publish

evals.prompt: How do I update my Walrus Site after making code changes?
evals.expected_output: Rebuild the frontend, then run site-builder update --epochs N dist/.
expectations: Instructs to rebuild first (npm run build)
Uses site-builder update (not publish) for subsequent deploys
Mentions that ws-resources.json contains the site object ID for updates
Includes --epochs flag to extend blob storage if needed

evals.prompt: My Walrus Site was working yesterday but now it shows 404. I didn't change anything.
evals.expected_output: Blobs likely expired. Run site-builder sitemap to check expiration dates. Re-publish with higher --epochs.
expectations: Suggests blob expiration as the likely cause
Recommends site-builder sitemap to check expiration dates
Instructs to re-publish with a higher --epochs value
Does NOT suggest code changes since the user said nothing changed
